group.name: Project — settings
group.note: One row per setting. Small, and deliberately schemaless: these are knobs, not material.

column.name: key
column.note: setting name; see the META_* constants in schema.rs

column.name: value
column.note: always text, parsed by the reader that cares

object.name: meta
object.note: Project-wide settings: schema version, resolution, default tuning, root phrase.

group.name: Tuning — definition, binder, frozen result
group.note: The three-level stack (R-501..R-506). The definition side stays exact: rules remain rules, ratios remain integer pairs. Materialization compiles a definition into a frozen table of frequencies, and *that* is playback truth — persisted, so a project sounds identical years later no matter what the builder has since learned. Everything downstream resolves through one materialization, which is the funnel a dynamic tuning (R-515) would use.

column.name: id
column.note: never reused; redo reinserts at this exact id

column.name: name
column.note: user-facing name, e.g. "16-ET"

column.name: description
column.note: prose; NULL on a derived row inherits its parent's

column.name: kind
column.note: 'equal' is the only rule kept in the schema, because its steps are irrational and cannot be stored exactly; everything else materializes into tuning_note rows

column.name: period_num
column.note: interval of equivalence as an exact ratio: 2/1 octave,

column.name: period_den
column.note: 3/1 Bohlen-Pierce; both NULL means aperiodic (R-502)

column.name: note_per_period
column.note: the modulus pitch-class logic uses; NULL when aperiodic

column.name: anchor_note
column.note: the note bound to anchor_freq (R-503); builtins use 60

column.name: anchor_freq
column.note: middle C for every builtin, so switching a phrase's tuning keeps its home pitch

column.name: note_min
column.note: materialization domain for kinds whose rule is unbounded;

column.name: note_max
column.note: NULL for an aperiodic table, whose rows are its domain

column.name: naming
column.note: R-508 scheme: letter, hex, near12; presentation only

column.name: origin
column.note: provenance (R-413): builtin, generated, derived, user

column.name: seed
column.note: JSON recipe: generator name, parameters, seed

column.name: parent_tuning_id
column.note: lineage for derived tunings, e.g. re-rooting (R-517 open)

column.name: extra
column.note: JSON escape valve; a field graduates to a column when it earns an index or a CHECK

object.name: tuning
object.note: A tuning definition: everything determining the note-number-to-frequency map.

column.name: tuning_id
column.note: the tuning these notes define

column.name: note_number
column.note: absolute; periodic tunings cover [anchor, anchor + N)

column.name: ratio_num
column.note: exact ratio from the anchor — a just fifth is 3/2,

column.name: ratio_den
column.note: never 701.955 cents

column.name: freq
column.note: or a direct frequency: "this frequency is this note", for measured instruments and hand-assigned pitches

object.name: tuning_note
object.note: Per-note data for a table tuning: one canonical period when periodic, the whole domain when not. Exact by construction (R-504).

column.name: id
column.note: what everything downstream resolves through

column.name: tuning_id
column.note: the definition this compiled from

column.name: ts
column.note: epoch milliseconds; carried in the resolved command so replay reproduces it rather than re-stamping

object.name: materialized_tuning_instance
object.note: The binder: names one materialization of one tuning, and nothing else. Deliberately pure — every column added here is a column every future dynamic-tuning mechanism must reckon with.

column.name: materialized_tuning_instance_id
column.note: which materialization this row belongs to

column.name: note_number
column.note: signed; dense tunings run well below the anchor

column.name: freq
column.note: hertz, strictly increasing with note number — the invariant nearest-note search rests on

object.name: materialized_tuning
object.note: The frozen result: playback truth. Uniform across every kind, so consumers never branch on how a tuning was defined.

group.name: Scale — shapes, not keys
group.note: A scale stores a shape and never a rooted result: the mask is relative to the tonic and the root is supplied at the use site, so "major" is one row serving every key and every 12-note tuning. Applicability is by modulus and is the only relationship enforced; whether a mask makes musical sense over a given tuning and root is idiomatic fit — advisory, never prohibited (R-509/R-510).

column.name: id
column.note: never reused, so an undone create can be redone

column.name: name
column.note: e.g. "Mavila (7)"

column.name: description
column.note: prose; what the shape is and where it comes from

column.name: note_per_period
column.note: the modulus a periodic mask belongs to: its structural parent, so one mask serves 12-ET and just intonation

column.name: tuning_id
column.note: aperiodic scales only: absolute subsets belong to one tuning. Exactly one of this and note_per_period is set

column.name: mask
column.note: JSON array: root-relative offsets when periodic, absolute note numbers when not

column.name: origin
column.note: provenance (R-413), as for tunings and phrases

column.name: seed
column.note: JSON recipe, for generated scales

column.name: parent_scale_id
column.note: lineage, e.g. a mode recorded against the mask it rotates

column.name: extra
column.note: JSON escape valve

object.name: scale
object.note: A named subset of pitch classes, or of note numbers when aperiodic.

group.name: Material — phrases, tracks, events, instances
group.note: The note model (R-001): a phrase is the unit of material, an instance places it in time with its own non-destructive play parameters, and editing a phrase affects every instance of it. There is one kind of track (R-425), holding both direct events and instances.

column.name: id
column.note: never reused; instances reference it, undo restores it

column.name: name
column.note: user-facing; the library is browsed by it

column.name: length_tick
column.note: the WINDOW: gates onsets and sets the loop stride. Events at or beyond it are dormant material — retained, silent — so narrowing is a non-destructive mute and widening reveals what was always there

column.name: tuning_id
column.note: the tuning its note numbers are read in (R-418); NULL falls back to the project default

column.name: scale_id
column.note: NULL is chromatic — there is no chromatic row (R-510)

column.name: root
column.note: the pitch class the mask is rooted on; overall root semantics are still open (R-517)

column.name: origin
column.note: recorded, generated, derived (R-413)

column.name: seed
column.note: JSON: seed, generator version, parameters

column.name: parent_phrase_id
column.note: the material this was derived from

column.name: extra
column.note: JSON escape valve; meter map and instrument binding live here until they earn columns (R-414)

object.name: phrase
object.note: A named container of events: the reusable unit of material (R-401).

column.name: id
column.note: never reused

column.name: phrase_id
column.note: the root phrase that owns it; multi-track sub-phrases are deferred, not foreclosed

column.name: name
column.note: user-facing track name

column.name: ord
column.note: sibling order

column.name: extra
column.note: JSON escape valve

object.name: track
object.note: An ordered container of events and instances (R-406). One kind only (R-425).

column.name: id
column.note: never reused; an undone removal restores rows at their ids

column.name: phrase_id
column.note: container: inside a phrase, XOR

column.name: track_id
column.note: directly on a track (R-406)

column.name: kind
column.note: open vocabulary: note, cc, articulation, audio (R-402)

column.name: at_tick
column.note: position in ticks at 5040 per quarter (R-003); seconds are derived only at the engine boundary

column.name: dur_tick
column.note: a note crossing the window edge rings out rather than being truncated at every loop seam

column.name: note_number
column.note: the pitch datum (R-002): a signed position in a tuning, not a frequency and not a MIDI note. NULL for unpitched kinds

column.name: velocity
column.note: 0..65535, the MIDI 2.0 domain; 7-bit values are translated at the MIDI boundary and the UI presents 0..127

column.name: extra
column.note: JSON escape valve; per-kind fields live here

object.name: event
object.note: One event: a note today, and a vocabulary open to controllers, articulation and audio without a schema change (R-402).

column.name: id
column.note: never reused

column.name: phrase_id
column.note: the material being referenced

column.name: track_id
column.note: container: on a track, XOR

column.name: parent_phrase_id
column.note: nested inside a parent phrase (R-407). Cycles are rejected in core

column.name: at_tick
column.note: where the instance starts in its container's time

column.name: offset_tick
column.note: where the window starts *within the material* — Vision's "play bars 8 to 16 of 32"

column.name: length_tick
column.note: the instance's own length, independent of the phrase's (R-405); NULL means the natural extent of loop_count iterations

column.name: loop_count
column.note: repetitions; the stride is the phrase's window length

column.name: transpose
column.note: chromatic transpose in note numbers, read in the material's tuning (R-423). Degree transposition is a separate, scale-relative verb

column.name: mute
column.note: non-destructive silence

column.name: extra
column.note: JSON escape valve; articulation template and timbre arc land here

object.name: phrase_instance
object.note: A placement of a phrase in time, carrying its own play parameters (R-404/405). All instances share the referenced material: edit the phrase and every instance follows.

column.name: id
column.note: never reused

column.name: phrase_id
column.note: the phrase whose map this belongs to (R-414)

column.name: at_tick
column.note: where the tempo takes effect

column.name: usec_per_quarter
column.note: microseconds per quarter note: MIDI-exact and integral, so the model never accumulates float drift. Seconds are derived at the engine only

object.name: tempo_point
object.note: One point in a phrase's tempo map.

group.name: History — the only write path
group.note: Model mutations are commands, and the journal records them in the same transaction as the rows they change (R-205). Undo does not rewind or delete entries; it appends a marker naming what it reversed. History therefore never lies, and the undo stack survives a restart for free.

column.name: seq
column.note: order of application

column.name: gesture
column.note: the undo unit; one gesture may hold several commands

column.name: kind
column.note: a command entry, or a marker recording that a gesture was reversed or reapplied

column.name: target_gesture
column.note: which gesture a marker refers to; NULL on command entries

column.name: ts
column.note: epoch milliseconds

column.name: command
column.note: the command's name, denormalized so history is filterable in plain SQL ("every set_tempo ever")

column.name: redo
column.note: JSON: the RESOLVED command, with executor-assigned ids and timestamps filled in, so replay reproduces exactly and never renumbers

column.name: undo
column.note: JSON: the inverse commands, computed when the command ran because removed rows cannot be re-derived later

object.name: journal
object.note: Append-only command history. Rows are never updated or deleted.

column.name: id
column.note: waypoint identity; nothing references it yet

column.name: journal_seq
column.note: history is folded up to here

column.name: ts
column.note: epoch milliseconds

column.name: state
column.note: reserved for the R-203 JSON at that point, for coarse time travel

object.name: snapshot
object.note: A compaction waypoint. Dormant: the retention policy is keep-everything, so nothing writes here yet (core-01). Present because trimming, if it ever happens, must leave a marker saying history before this point is gone.

group.name: Realization — the executable specification
group.note: The view is the model's specification (§6f-bis): any optimized replacement must match it, and it remains the oracle after it stops being the implementation. Structural laws are what make it well-defined — the cycle prohibition is its termination guarantee.

object.name: v_realized
object.note: The arrangement as it will actually sound: direct events on a track, unioned with events reached through an instance, with placement, looping, windowing and transposition applied.  The window gates ONSETS. An event starting outside [offset, offset + length) is dormant material, and a note whose duration crosses the window edge rings out rather than being cut at every loop seam.
object.sql:
CREATE VIEW v_realized AS
SELECT e.track_id            AS track_id,
       e.kind                AS kind,
       e.at_tick             AS at_tick,
       e.dur_tick            AS dur_tick,
       e.note_number         AS note_number,
       e.velocity            AS velocity,
       ph.tuning_id          AS tuning_id,
       NULL                  AS phrase_instance_id
FROM event e
JOIN track t   ON t.id = e.track_id
JOIN phrase ph ON ph.id = t.phrase_id
WHERE e.track_id IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT i.track_id,
       e.kind,
       i.at_tick + (l.value - 1) * p.length_tick + (e.at_tick - i.offset_tick),
       e.dur_tick,
       e.note_number + i.transpose,
       e.velocity,
       p.tuning_id,
       i.id
FROM phrase_instance i
JOIN phrase p ON p.id = i.phrase_id
CROSS JOIN generate_series(1, i.loop_count) l  -- a correlated table-valued argument: an
JOIN event e ON e.phrase_id = i.phrase_id
WHERE i.track_id IS NOT NULL
  AND i.mute = 0
  AND e.at_tick >= i.offset_tick
  AND e.at_tick - i.offset_tick < p.length_tick
  AND (l.value - 1) * p.length_tick + (e.at_tick - i.offset_tick)
      < COALESCE(i.length_tick, i.loop_count * p.length_tick)
